claims.claim: The agent model (AM) and world model (WM) must be kept functionally distinct: AM decides what to do, WM predicts what will happen.
evidence: Formal factorization: p^π_μ(a_t,s_{t+1},...,s_T|s_t) = ∏ p_π(a_k|s_k) · p_μ(s_{k+1}|s_k,a_k)
Equation (1) decomposition into agent factor and universe factor
§4.5: Separate training signals — WM uses prediction error, AM uses reward/goal-directed feedback
Contrast with recent collapsed models: DreamZero (Ye et al. 2026), Cosmos (NVIDIA 2026a), Li (2026)

claims.claim: Agentic vs. Agentive: Agentic systems achieve competence through external scaffolding; Agentive systems derive capabilities endogenously.
evidence: Agentic: 'competence resides primarily in the engineering around a given reasoning model'
Agentive: 'derive their capabilities endogenously (maintaining long-term goals, evolving self-identity, simulating future possibilities, regulating when to reason, learning better behaviors)'
Five-dimensional spectrum framework (goals, identity, decision-making, self-regulation, learning)
claims.source_section: 1 Introduction, 2 The Boundary Between Agentic and Agentive Systems

claims.claim: Fast-slow learning (parameter + identity updates) dominates slow-only learning in regret.
evidence: Theorem 1: Regret^{fast-slow}_K ≤ Regret^{std}_K - Ω(∑_{k=1}^K N_k)
Per-step gain ε̄ = inf_{k,t} E[Δ_t] > 0 from identity revision
Proof: Three-step argument — per-step gain (A1), within-round aggregation (Step 2), cross-round compounding via monotonic update operator (A2, Step 3)
Assumptions: A1(a) identity revision closes gap to optimal, A1(b) closer identity → higher value, A2 monotone update operator

claims.claim: World-model-based planning improves any baseline policy given a reasonably accurate world model.
evidence: Theorem 2: For TV-bounded world model (error ≤ ε), ∃ π_mix = φ(f,π,ε) such that V^g_{π_mix} ≥ V^g_π
Proof: Simulation Lemma → model error bound 2γR_maxε/(1-γ)² = ε_model
Mixed policy follows π*_f only when its value exceeds π's by 2ε_model margin
Performance Difference Lemma ensures true improvement in real environment

claims.claim: Current agentic systems cannot scale to long-horizon tasks because they require human-supplied step-by-step goals.
evidence: Examples: Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, π-series, Helix all receive short-term instructions
Goal disappears after each interaction; new goal required for further action
Impossibility of scaling to tasks like 'make wine over a year'
Alternative: hierarchical decomposition with learned module δ and hierarchical world model f
claims.source_section: §4.1 Goal: From Step-by-Step Instruction to Hierarchical Decomposition

claims.claim: Identity in current systems is externally specified via system prompts and harness engineering, which precludes genuine autonomy.
evidence: Identity implemented as hand-written system prompts (role, tools, constraints)
MCP (Anthropic 2024) and Agent Skills (Anthropic 2025b) as examples of 'harness engineering'
Cannot adapt to new environments without human re-engineering
Alternative: fast-slow update with identity evolver ι
claims.source_section: §4.2 Identity: From Harness Engineering to Adaptive Self-Models

claims.claim: Black-box end-to-end policies conflate internal compute with planning; chain-of-thought is not grounded in real-world dynamics.
evidence: CoT 'describes possible futures but these descriptions are not grounded in a model that predicts state transitions from observations'
'prediction based on narrative plausibility (token probability) rather than real-world consistency'
Cites Florence and Generalist AI Team (2026) as advocating emergent-planning hypothesis
Contrast: world model provides grounded counterfactual engine for course-of-action evaluation
claims.source_section: §4.3 Decision-Making: From Black-Box Policies to Simulative Reasoning

claims.claim: Current approaches to self-regulation are insufficient: unconstrained RL leads to over-/under-thinking; fixed workflows are rigid.
evidence: Su et al. (2025) on underthinking/overthinking in LLMs
Gema et al. (2025) on inverse scaling in test-time compute
GPT-5 backlash (Newton 2025) and Claude Opus 4.7 backlash (Hwang 2026) over invisible model picker
Alternative: learned configurator κ governing deliberation mode allocation

claims.claim: Even 'AI trains AI' systems keep the learning loop external — the agent does not govern its own training.
evidence: 'In virtually all such AI training AI systems, the learning process itself remains external'
Training decisions (when to learn, what data, how long, when to stop) are made by humans
Self-directed learning = configurator κ governs learning as part of ongoing decision-making
Theorem 4 (informal): mixture of simulated+real experience dominates real-only training
claims.source_section: §2.6 Learning, §4.5 Learning: From Human-Scheduled Pipelines to Self-Directed Learning

claims.claim: The GIC architecture provides layered transparency: each agency-relevant capacity is explicit, inspectable, and auditable.
evidence: δ (goal decomposition) can be audited for undesirable subgoals before execution
ι (identity evolution) can be monitored over time for appropriate self-model
f (world model) predictions can be inspected for consistency with reality
κ (configurator) decisions can be audited for proportional deliberation
Learning decisions can be reviewed for competence gaps

claims.claim: Goal-oriented data (extended purposeful activity with goal annotations) is the highest-leverage investment for training general-purpose agent models.
evidence: Example: video of 'leaving apartment → taking elevator → hailing cab → arriving at airport' structured by goal 'fly to Paris'
Goal annotation transforms noisy activity streams into training signals for multi-scale planning
'Curating and scaling goal-oriented datasets is among the highest-leverage investments'

claims.claim: In GIC, harmful behavior decomposes entirely into goal misspecification or component imperfection — no emergent misalignment.
evidence: Goal g is exogenous — 'no mechanism for GIC to generate its own terminal objectives'
Harmful subgoal → poorly trained δ, not emergent fundamental misalignment
Identity i_t is instrumental and subordinate to g
Configurator regulates how, not what
'For a sufficiently well-trained system, harmful behavior converges to zero unless the goal itself is wrong'

claims.claim: Building agents with transparent architectures (like GIC) is itself a safety intervention — the alternative is opaque systems where failures cannot be diagnosed.
evidence: Aviation analogy: 'Pilots crash during training; the response was not to ban pilot training but to develop simulators, staged curricula, instructor oversight'
'The choice is whether they are developed within transparent architectures where failures can be isolated and corrected, or within opaque ones where they cannot'
Agent trains primarily in world model before real deployment

claims.claim: The collaborative/competitive social coordination in current 'multi-agent' systems is externally orchestrated, not emergent from agents' own decisions.
evidence: AutoGen (Wu et al. 2023) and OpenAI Swarm specify team membership, protocols, roles externally
'Such systems are better understood as a single scaffolded system consisting of a federation of tasks'
Agentive: coordination emerges as internal decision of participating agents

claims.claim: GIC training follows a three-phase curriculum: ground school (pretraining), simulator hours (simulative RL), first flights (real-world deployment/refinement).
evidence: Phase 1: Agent model from LLM, world model via GLP, critic from reward data, policy from demos
Phase 2: RL within world model — builds System I, II, III competence
Phase 3: Real-world refinement — sim-to-real gap correction, identity evolution
Pilot training analogy throughout
